subject: Physics
title: Does the Moon Exist When Nobody Looks?
description: A micro-lesson that makes Does the Moon Exist When Nobody Looks? usable.

hook.text: Einstein asked: does the Moon exist only when someone looks at it? Quantum mechanics complicates things.

buildup.text: In the quantum world, particles don't have definite properties until measured. Is the same true for moons?

discovery.text: Decoherence says large objects like the Moon interact with so many particles that they're always 'measured.'

twist.text: But the fundamental question remains: does reality need an observer, or does it exist independently?

climax.text: After a century, physicists still disagree. Quantum reality's deepest question remains open.

punchline.text: The Moon probably exists. Quantum mechanics just can't promise.

quiz.question: Why does quantum mechanics complicate the question of whether the Moon exists unobserved?
options: Quantum objects don't have definite properties until measured
The Moon is too far to observe accurately
Gravity prevents quantum effects on the Moon
